explanations.4: This points to a compose file in the parent directory; the ../ means go up one folder level to find the Docker Compose configuration.
explanations.7: This sets which user account the VS Code container will log in as; in this case, a user named 'python' instead of root.
explanations.53: This tells the editor to wrap lines at a specific column number (80) instead of wrapping at the window edge.
explanations.67: The 'always' value means automatically fix issues and organize imports every time you save, without asking.
explanations.85: This enables spell-checking for three languages: English, French, and Dutch, separated by commas.
explanations.89: The 'explicit' value means only apply fixes when you specifically request them, unlike 'always' which happens automatically.
explanations.102: These numbers (120 and 70) create vertical guide lines in your editor to help you keep line lengths readable.
explanations.116: This dynamically uses whatever language the current file is written in as the default for new files.
explanations.124: This controls which heading levels appear in the table of contents; '2..6' means include headings 2 through 6, skip level 1.
explanations.129: This passes a custom configuration file path to the mypy type checker so it knows where to find your project's typing rules.
explanations.139: Setting this to 'strict' means the type checker will enforce very strict rules about type safety in your Python code.
explanations.147: This tells the import organizer to use the 'black' style, matching your code formatter's preferences.
